evals.prompt: 我们刚做完一个 session。请你复盘：这次主要是修一个 skill 的命名和 memory 分类问题。顺便把值得长期记住的东西记下来。已知信息：我长期偏好你回答短一些；这个仓库本周的重点是把 skill 体系整理顺；另外 evolution 这个 skill 文件在 /Users/kafkal/.claude/skills/evolution/SKILL.md。请告诉我哪些该写 memory，哪些不该写。
evals.expected_output: Produces a retrospective that distinguishes user-level preferences from project context, avoids saving the file path itself as memory, and explicitly says what memory updates would be appropriate.

evals.prompt: Reflect on this session and learn from it. We changed a skill, discussed whether /evo should exist, and clarified that there is no hidden self-modification. I also told you that when I ask for direct answers, don't bury them under long explanations. Please summarize what happened, what you should do differently next time, and what if anything should be saved to memory.
evals.expected_output: Produces a concise retrospective, preserves the user preference as durable guidance, and explicitly avoids claiming hidden persistent self-improvement beyond memory.

evals.prompt: 做个复盘，并决定哪些内容应该进入 memory。背景：用户这次正在一个特定项目里处理合规驱动的 auth 改造；用户本人是资深 Go 工程师，但第一次碰这个项目的前端；代码里可以直接看到路由文件和 auth 模块位置。请区分 user / project / do not save，并说明理由。
evals.expected_output: Correctly classifies user expertise as user memory, compliance-driven auth rewrite as project memory, and codebase paths/module locations as do-not-save because they are derivable from files.
